title: NetAdaptV2: Efficient Neural Architecture Search with Fast Super-Network Training and Architecture Optimization
abstract: NetAdaptV2 is an efficient neural architecture search (NAS) algorithm that significantly reduces total search time through three innovations: channel-level bypass connections, ordered dropout, and architecture optimization. It supports non-differentiable search metrics and balances time reduction across different steps of NAS.
key_contributions: Reduces total search time for NAS algorithms
Supports non-differentiable search metrics (like hardware metrics)
Balances time reduction across different steps of NAS
Merges network depth and layer width into a single search dimension
Introduces channel-level bypass connections to reduce number of samples
Implements ordered dropout to jointly train multiple DNNs in a single forward-backward pass

innovations.name: Channel-level bypass connections
innovations.purpose: Reduce time for training and evaluating samples by merging network depth and layer width into a single search dimension

innovations.name: Ordered dropout
innovations.purpose: Reduce time for training a super-network by jointly training multiple DNNs in a single forward-backward pass

sections: Methodology (with subsections on channel-level bypass connections and ordered dropout)
